findings.title: — Hardcoded credentials, passwords, API keys, tokens, or TLS InsecureSkipVerify:
findings.description: The code contains a hardcoded string that could be sensitive information.

findings.title: — No Hardcoded Secrets or Credentials
findings.description: The HTTP client is created with `InsecureSkipVerify` set to true, which disables TLS verification. This is a security risk and should never be used in production.

findings.title: — fmt.Println used instead of structured logger
findings.description: — fmt.Println used instead of structured logger

findings.title: — context.Context is not the first function parameter
findings.description: The `context.Context` should be the first parameter in any function that uses it.

findings.title: — Always wrap errors with context
findings.description: The error is returned without wrapping it using `fmt.Errorf` with `%w`.

findings.title: — Always wrap errors with context
findings.description: Bare error returns lose the call chain context. Use fmt.Errorf with %w to wrap errors so the full trace is preserved for debugging.

findings.title: — Goroutine launched with infinite loop and no ctx.Done() / done channel
findings.description: — Goroutine launched with infinite loop and no ctx.Done() / done channel

findings.title: — fmt.Printf used instead of structured logger
findings.description: The use of `fmt.Println` for logging is less flexible and harder to parse than a structured logger.

findings.title: SEVERITY — `panic(...)` used for a recoverable error condition
findings.description: The function uses `panic` to handle an error that is recoverable. This can lead to unexpected crashes and should be avoided.

findings.title: SEVERITY — Always wrap errors with context
findings.description: The error returned from `dblib.Delete` is not wrapped with context using `fmt.Errorf`.
